content.text: I found the dialog service interface/implementation and existing dialog tests; I’ll inspect those next to understand the overload patterns.

content.content:
1	// Copyright (c) 2019 Blazored (https://github.com/Blazored)
2	// Copyright (c) 2020 Jonny Larsson (https://github.com/MudBlazor/MudBlazor)

7	using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;
8	using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components;
9	
10	namespace MudBlazor
11	{
12	    /// <summary>
13	    /// A service for managing <see cref="MudDialog"/> components.
14	    /// </summary>
15	    /// <remarks>
16	    /// This service requires a <see cref="MudDialogProvider"/> in your layout page.
17	    /// </remarks>
18	    public interface IDialogService
19	    {
20	
21	        /// <summary>
22	        /// Occurs when a new dialog instance is created.
23	        /// </summary>
24	        event Func<IDialogReference, Task> DialogInstanceAddedAsync;
25	
26	        /// <summary>
27	        /// Occurs when a request is made to close a dialog.
28	        /// </summary>
29	        event Action<IDialogReference, DialogResult?>? OnDialogCloseRequested;
30	
31	        /// <summary>
32	        /// Displays a dialog.
33	        /// </summary>
34	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
35	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
36	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>() where TComponent : IComponent;
37	
38	        /// <summary>
39	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title.
40	        /// </summary>
41	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
42	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
43	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
44	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title) where TComponent : IComponent;
45	
46	        /// <summary>
47	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and options.
48	        /// </summary>
49	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
50	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
51	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
52	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
53	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogOptions options) where TComponent : IComponent;
54	
55	        /// <summary>
56	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and parameters.
57	        /// </summary>
58	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
59	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
60	        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
61	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
62	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters) where TComponent : IComponent;
63	
64	        /// <summary>
65	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title, parameters, and options.
66	        /// </summary>
67	        /// <typeparam name="TComponent">The dialog to display.</typeparam>
68	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
69	        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
70	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
71	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
72	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync<[DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] TComponent>(string? title, DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions? options) where TComponent : IComponent;
73	
74	        /// <summary>
75	        /// Displays a dialog.
76	        /// </summary>
77	        /// <param name="component">The dialog to display.</param>
78	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
79	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component);
80	
81	        /// <summary>
82	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title.
83	        /// </summary>
84	        /// <param name="component">The dialog to display.</param>
85	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
86	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
87	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title);
88	
89	        /// <summary>
90	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and options.
91	        /// </summary>
92	        /// <param name="component">The dialog to display.</param>
93	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
94	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
95	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
96	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogOptions options);
97	
98	        /// <summary>
99	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title and parameters.
100	        /// </summary>
101	        /// <param name="component">The dialog to display.</param>
102	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
103	        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
104	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
105	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogParameters parameters);
106	
107	        /// <summary>
108	        /// Displays a dialog with a custom title, parameters, and options.
109	        /// </summary>
110	        /// <param name="component">The dialog to display.</param>
111	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
112	        /// <param name="parameters">The custom parameters to set within the dialog.</param>
113	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.</param>
114	        /// <returns>A reference to the dialog.</returns>
115	        Task<IDialogReference> ShowAsync([DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.All)] Type component, string? title, DialogParameters parameters, DialogOptions options);
116	
117	        /// <summary>
118	        /// Creates a reference to a dialog.
119	        /// </summary>
120	        /// <returns>The dialog reference.</returns>
121	        IDialogReference CreateReference();
122	
123	        /// <summary>
124	        /// Shows a simple dialog with a title, message, and up to three custom buttons.
125	        /// </summary>
126	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
127	        /// <param name="message">The message displayed under the title.</param>
128	        /// <param name="yesText">The text of the "Yes" button.  Defaults to <c>OK</c>.</param>
129	        /// <param name="noText">The text of the "No" button.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
130	        /// <param name="cancelText">The text of the "Cancel" button.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
131	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
132	        /// <returns>Returns <c>null</c> if the <c>Cancel</c> button was clicked, <c>true</c> if the <c>Yes</c> button was clicked, or <c>false</c> if the <c>No</c> button was clicked.</returns>
133	        Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, string message, string yesText = "OK",
134	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null);
135	
136	        /// <summary>
137	        /// Shows a simple dialog with a title, HTML message, and up to three custom buttons.
138	        /// </summary>
139	        /// <param name="title">The text at the top of the dialog.</param>
140	        /// <param name="markupMessage">The HTML message displayed under the title.</param>
141	        /// <param name="yesText">The text of the "Yes" button.  Defaults to <c>OK</c>.</param>
142	        /// <param name="noText">The text of the "No" button.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
143	        /// <param name="cancelText">The text of the "Cancel" button.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
144	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
145	        /// <returns>Returns <c>null</c> if the <c>Cancel</c> button was clicked, <c>true</c> if the <c>Yes</c> button was clicked, or <c>false</c> if the <c>No</c> button was clicked.</returns>
146	        Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(string? title, MarkupString markupMessage, string yesText = "OK",
147	            string? noText = null, string? cancelText = null, DialogOptions? options = null);
148	
149	        /// <summary>
150	        /// Shows a simple dialog with custom options.
151	        /// </summary>
152	        /// <param name="messageBoxOptions">The options for the message box.</param>
153	        /// <param name="options">The custom display options for the dialog.  Defaults to <c>null</c>.</param>
154	        /// <returns>Returns <c>null</c> if the <c>Cancel</c> button was clicked, <c>true</c> if the <c>Yes</c> button was clicked, or <c>false</c> if the <c>No</c> button was clicked.</returns>
155	        Task<bool?> ShowMessageBoxAsync(MessageBoxOptions messageBoxOptions, DialogOptions? options = null);
156	
157	        /// <summary>
158	        /// Hides an existing dialog.
159	        /// </summary>
160	        /// <param name="dialog">The reference of the dialog to hide.</param>
161	        void Close(IDialogReference dialog);
162	
163	        /// <summary>
164	        /// Hides an existing dialog.
165	        /// </summary>
166	        /// <param name="dialog">The reference of the dialog to hide.</param>
167	        /// <param name="result">The result to include.</param>
168	        void Close(IDialogReference dialog, DialogResult? result);
169	    }
170	}
171

content.text:
Implemented the requested overload.

Changes made:
- Added `IDialogService.ShowAsync<TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters)` to `IDialogService`.
- Implemented the overload in `DialogService`, delegating to the existing overload with `string.Empty` as the title and default dialog options.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test proving `service.ShowAsync<DialogWithParameters>(parameters)` failed to compile before the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary test passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Ran an existing nearby dialog test successfully:
  - `AsyncDialogShouldNotOverwriteParameters` passed.

No commits were created.

result:
Implemented the requested overload.

Changes made:
- Added `IDialogService.ShowAsync<TComponent>(DialogParameters<TComponent> parameters)` to `IDialogService`.
- Implemented the overload in `DialogService`, delegating to the existing overload with `string.Empty` as the title and default dialog options.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test proving `service.ShowAsync<DialogWithParameters>(parameters)` failed to compile before the fix.
- Confirmed the temporary test passed after the fix.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Ran an existing nearby dialog test successfully:
  - `AsyncDialogShouldNotOverwriteParameters` passed.

No commits were created.
